name: Ansoff Matrix
summary: A framework for choosing growth strategies based on whether you are targeting new or existing markets with new or existing products.
whenToUseIt: When deciding how to grow: should you sell more to existing customers, enter new markets, build new products, or diversify?
steps: Map your current position (existing product + existing market = market penetration)
Evaluate market development (existing product + new market)
Evaluate product development (new product + existing market)
Evaluate diversification (new product + new market — highest risk)
Choose the quadrant with the best risk-adjusted return for your stage
example: A B2B SaaS company at $1M ARR should focus on market penetration before diversifying. Expanding to a new vertical (market development) is lower risk than building a completely new product (diversification).
